title: melampyrum sylvaticum subsp. aestivale
intro: Melampyrum sylvaticum subsp. aestivale, also known as the Summer Bitterwort or Yellow Archangel, is a plant species within the family Orobanchaceae, characterized by its yellow or greenish-yellow flowers and its ability to obtain nutrients by parasitizing other plants. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Melampyrum sylvaticum subsp. aestivale are said to include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential use in treating digestive issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
